methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

data.narrative:
Zeno Power is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $50M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 3 private peers at identical stage and sector (Aalyria, Hubble Network, and Sift) and 5 public Space & Aerospace companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL), though public comparables cannot be directly applied without revenue disclosure.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the company's $50M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $95M. Peer-median analysis anchors to the three same-stage, same-sector private comparables, which show a median valuation of $400M across a range of $274M to $1.3B. The dispersion reflects heterogeneity within the space sector; Aalyria commands a $1.3B valuation while Sift sits at $274M.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $81M to $460M, with a median of $248M. The wide range reflects material uncertainty around market positioning relative to peers. The stage-progression method yields the floor estimate, while peer medians anchor the central case. Key uncertainties include Zeno Power's technical differentiation relative to Aalyria and Hubble Network, capital efficiency trajectory, and customer traction—none of which the current dataset captures.
